key_insight: A physics-informed residual neural network can accurately model hot deformation and DRX in a Mo-rich titanium alloy while preserving constitutive consistency.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical surrogate for thermomechanical process design and optimization in titanium alloy forming, where flow stress and recrystallization behavior strongly affect manufacturability.
method: Stacked residual PINN with a shared encoder trained on experimental flow-stress curves
Enhanced model enforces thermomechanical constraints such as strain hardening, DRV, thermal softening, and post-peak softening
DRX-aware variant adds dual outputs plus JMAK-Avrami, DRX saturation, and Arrhenius-based regularization
evidence_signal: The DRX-aware STAR-PINN achieved RMSE 11.69 MPa, MAE 4.83 MPa, R2 0.9850, and cross-validated RMSE 12.47 ± 0.26 MPa on data spanning 800–1050 °C and 0.01–10 s^-1.
watch_item: The model’s reliability may depend on how well the training data covers extreme temperatures, strain rates, and DRX regimes outside the experimental envelope.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Residual Deep Learning for Constitutive Modeling of Hot Deformation and Dynamic Recrystallization in a Mo-Rich $α+β$ Titanium Alloy
